description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in gray shingles. The front facade features a prominent gabled section with large rectangular windows and a main entry door, flanked by two brick chimneys on either end of the roof. The house sits on a rectangular plot with a paved walkway and driveway on one side, and a brown yard or garden area on the other. The windows are symmetrically arranged on the front, with additional smaller windows on the sides and rear. The structure includes a small attached outbuilding or garage at the back, with a flat roof and a separate door. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century suburban homes, with simple lines and functional details.
description: A two-story brick house with a steep gray gable roof, symmetrical front windows, and a small attached outbuilding, set on a rectangular plot with paved and garden areas.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, large windows, and both chimneys, with the paved walkway and driveway visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the brick wall, side windows, and the attached outbuilding at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the house, the flat-roofed outbuilding, and the brown yard area.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the side entrance to the outbuilding, side windows, and the transition between paved and garden areas.
attribute_entryway_design: Central front door with a small step, flanked by large windows and sheltered by the gabled roof, no porch or columns.
